title: Class Garden Build
goal: Plan and build a small classroom garden before open house day.

risks.title: Rain might delay planting
risks.description: Prepare covers and an indoor seed tray backup.

response.label: Weather cover ready
response.outcome: The covers protected the seedlings from the worst rain.

risks.title: Some plants might not grow well
risks.description: Check which plants fit the classroom light and season.

response.label: Plant fit checked
response.outcome: The plant research helped the team recover quickly.

risks.title: Tools might be hard to share
risks.description: Create a tool rotation before building starts.

response.label: Tool rotation used
response.outcome: The rotation kept building fair and organized.

events.title: Heavy rain is coming
events.body: Rain may hit before the seeds settle into the planters.

choices.label: Cover the planters
choices.outcome: The seedlings stay safer, but setup takes extra attention.
choices.lesson: Small risk responses can protect a lot of project value.

choices.label: Move planting indoors for now
choices.outcome: The team avoids the rain and can transplant later.
choices.lesson: Changing the method can keep the goal alive.

choices.label: Keep the original plan
choices.outcome: The schedule stays simple, but seedlings may struggle.
choices.lesson: Ignoring clear risk signals can hurt quality.

events.title: Some plants are wilting
events.body: A few seedlings look weak after the first week.

choices.label: Check water and sunlight
choices.outcome: The team finds the likely cause and adjusts the care plan.
choices.lesson: Root cause thinking helps solve problems without guessing.

choices.label: Buy replacement plants
choices.outcome: The garden looks better quickly, but it costs more.
choices.lesson: Extra budget can rescue quality when used thoughtfully.

choices.label: Hide the weak plants
choices.outcome: The display looks cleaner, but the team misses a learning chance.
choices.lesson: Covering up problems does not improve the system.

events.title: Two teammates need the same tools
events.body: Building and cleanup both need the same gloves and trowels.

choices.label: Create a tool rotation
choices.outcome: The team shares fairly and keeps work moving.

choices.label: Let the fastest person go first
choices.outcome: One task moves quickly, but others feel stuck.
choices.lesson: Local speed can still hurt overall flow.

choices.label: Wait until everyone is free
choices.outcome: No one argues, but the schedule loses time.
choices.lesson: Avoiding conflict can still create delivery risk.

events.title: Teacher asks for clearer labels
events.body: The teacher likes the garden but says visitors may not know what each plant is.

choices.label: Make simple plant labels
choices.lesson: Good communication makes project value easier to see.

choices.label: Explain the plants during open house
choices.outcome: The team saves money, but someone must be ready to present.
choices.lesson: Service and support can be part of delivery.

choices.label: Skip labels
choices.outcome: The team avoids extra work, but the garden is harder to understand.
choices.lesson: Finishing the build is not the same as making it useful.
